The _run closure content is fully contained within ID 421 (Build method). Teaching the closure in isolation then teaching the full method in 421 creates a near-duplicate where 421 supersedes 418 entirely.

The _run closure content is fully contained within ID 422 (BuildFromTriples method); 422 supersedes 419. D5=2 because input is 12 lines but the for loop has no error path making the transformation mostly mechanical (no extractor call, no error handling pattern to teach).

REJECT: `var done chan struct{}` declares a nil channel. The goroutine then calls `close(done)` which panics at runtime on a nil channel. The select/receive on `<-done` would also block forever. D1=1 (runtime panic). D2=2 because the timeout semantics are approximated via WaitGroup rather than direct thread.join behavior.

Same goroutine-spawn + VersionedRef-update pattern as ID 421 (Build). Different input type (pre-extracted triples vs chunks+extractor) and no error path, but pattern is structurally near-identical. D6=2.

Input is a 5-line snippet extracted from the middle of a larger function; not a self-contained function. D5=1 (< 10 lines). D3=2: instruction says 'emit a self-contained Go function wrapping these loops' but the input is an incomplete code fragment requiring surrounding context to understand the types, making it ambiguous for a model without that context.
Input is 8 lines with no statement-level branching — pure struct field assignments and two append calls. Too simple for 14B tier.
assembleRows nested loop is essentially the same for-range-bySubject pattern already translated in ID 438 (SaveToDB), just without the namespace lookup. One dimension scores 2, rest are 3 — Accept.

parseDescriptions and parseAliases logic is already present inline inside ID 435 (parseEntityAnchored). Near-identical transformation pattern already covered — D6=2. One dimension scores 2, rest are 3 — Accept.

persistAssociations call passes data (map[string]interface{}) as second arg but the function defined in ID 446 expects associations []map[string]interface{} — type mismatch causes compile failure. Additionally, len(data["associations"].([]interface{})) is an unsafe type assertion that will panic at runtime if ToDict() returns a different concrete type.

R04 cited as 'list built via nested for loops instead of comprehension' but Python input already uses explicit append-in-for-loops, not a comprehension. No actual comprehension-to-loop conversion required. Minor D4 issue.

R10 cited as 'implicit match call -> no loop but calls into ranging methods' but run_ner, get_association, and chunks_containing_entity have no for-loops at all. R10 has no visible instance in the input.

D4=2: R09 (slice syntax) cited but no Python negative-index or slice syntax exists in the input. D6=2: _split_tokens logic fully recurs in ID 466 compound example.

Input is 9 non-empty lines (< 10 threshold). can_handle is a one-liner; fetch delegates to FetchMessages with a single nil check. Too simple for 14B tier.

Reject. attributevalue.UnmarshalMap(rawItem) called with 1 argument and return value assigned directly to item. AWS SDK v2 signature is UnmarshalMap(m map[string]types.AttributeValue, out interface{}) error — requires 2 args and error check. Non-compiling code; item type would be error not map[string]interface{}, making subsequent range over item and map assignment both wrong.

Near-identical transformation pattern to ID 497 (CosmosCrawler.Fetch + GetFieldNames): same cache-lookup error pattern and identical set-comprehension-with-aliases to sorted slice pattern. Only difference is class name and one fewer normalized-names addition.
